title: Recover incident-report retrieval for lost-sales questions
description: RAG eval miss lost_sales_estimate: 'lost sales' phrasing retrieves the Electronics deep-dive instead of 11_Seasonal_Demand_Incident_Report / 12_Inventory_Shortage_Root_Cause_Analysis. Proposed repair: extend the retrieval query normalization synonyms (lost sales -> stockout, demand incident, shortage) in agents/rag_agent.py _normalize_retrieval_query, then re-run evals/rag_eval.py; adopt only if hit rate improves with no new misses.

history.note: awaiting before/after eval run of the proposed synonym change

synonym expansion in _normalize_retrieval_query; eval 98%->100% hit rate, 0 misses, no regressions

title: Route corpus-inventory questions to documents; recover from empty SQL
description: Trace a737959ac7b04c7f: 'sell-through rate for SKU FOOD-5001' was rule-routed to sql_only ('rate' keyword), returning 'n/a / 0 transactions' with no confidence. Repair (commit 6234501e): SQL negative patterns + corpus_operations RAG vocabulary in config/data_inventory.py, plus a bounded one-step RAG fallback in both engines when SQL executes clean but matches no data (_sql_result_has_no_data).

eval_before.live_check: confidence UNKNOWN, route sql_only, answer 'Sell through rate: n/a, Transactions analyzed: 0', no citations

eval_after.live_check: confidence HIGH, route rag_only, answer '90.3%' citing warehouse_inventory_export.csv + business_glossary.md
eval_after.regressions: none — full suite green, RAG eval still 100% hit rate

history.note: repair implemented on branch; awaiting verification evidence
6 new detector tests + live before/after payloads; adoption awaits Prem's branch review
